recalls.Description: Tainoki Fine Furniture has issued a recall for height-adjustable swivel office chairs intended for desk use. These chairs come with a padded seat, backrest, headrest, and two padded armrests, all supported by chrome-finished metal bars. The recall includes specific models: the Noah Office Chair (model M7016O) available in Cream, Jiffy, French Roast, and Black; the Owen Office Chair (model M7004O) in Cream, Black, Merlin, Jiffy, and Wade; and the Warren Office Chair (model M7074O) in Taupe and Justin's. Model numbers can be found on a label located on the underside of the chair seat. Each chair is mounted on a five-star chrome metal base with five black rolling casters.
recalls.Title: Tainoki Fine Furniture Recalls Office Chairs Due to Fall Hazard Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For more information, consumers can contact Tainoki Fine Furniture at 888-698-2466 from 10 a.m. to 7 p.m. PT, Monday through Friday, via email at hello@tainoki.com, or visit https://www.tainoki.com/recall. Alternatively, consumers can go to https://www.tainoki.com and click on 'Recall' at the bottom of the page.

recalls.Description: Cranach Hardware has issued a recall for its plastic tip restraint kits, commonly known as furniture straps, due to a significant tip-over hazard. These kits include two white plastic brackets for securing furniture to a wall, a zip tie, screws, and drywall anchors. Over time, the plastic components may break or degrade, increasing the risk of furniture tipping over, which poses a danger, especially to young children and elderly individuals. Testing by the CPSC indicated that these kits do not meet the ASTM F3096-23 safety standard for tip restraints.

RH is recalling several models of its Byron furniture due to a potential injury risk. The affected products include the Byron Reeded Stone dining tables and desks, available in various lengths and finishes such as Italian Classico Travertine, Brown Emperador Marble, and Spanish Nero Marquina Marble. recalls.Description:
At Home has issued a recall for its Tie Dye Fur Toy Trunk Storage Chests, available in two sizes. The larger chest measures approximately 32-½" x 16-½" x 21", while the smaller chest measures around 27" x 13-½" x 16". These chests are constructed from MDF wood and are covered in a vibrant multi-color tie dye faux fur fabric. The absence of a lid support feature and ventilation holes creates significant entrapment and suffocation hazards for children.

Consumers are urged to stop using these toy storage chests immediately. They can return the items to any At Home location for a complete refund. This recall affects products sold from September 2023 through February 2024, priced between $50 and $60.

recalls.Description: Dania Furniture is recalling the Hayden bookcase, which features six storage cubbies and is constructed from brown wood with three sliding white doors. This bookcase measures 35.5 inches wide, 16 inches deep, and 73 inches tall. Each unit has a label on the back displaying the product name and SKU number LB2225/A.
recalls.Title: Dania Furniture Recalls Hayden Bookcase Due to Tip-Over Risks; Child Fatality Reported
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact Dania Furniture at 844-722-6347 from 9 a.m. to 6 p.m. PT, Monday through Friday, or email ProductSafetyHotline@interline.com. More information is available online at https://daniafurniture.com/pages/safety-recalls or by visiting https://daniafurniture.com/ and selecting 'Safety Recalls' at the top of the page.

Images.Caption: Recalled Hayden Bookcase
Recalled Hayden Bookcase label

Injuries.Name: In August 2023, Dania Furniture received one report of a tip-over incident with an unanchored bookcase that resulted in the death of a 4-year-old child.

Retailers.Name: Approximately 940 units of this bookcase were sold exclusively at Dania Furniture locations across the country and online at https://daniafurniture.com/ from November 2017 through February 2024 for around $370.

Distributors.Name: Dania Furniture, of Boise, Idaho

Hazards.Name: The bookcase poses a risk of tipping over if not secured to the wall, which can lead to serious injuries or fatalities, especially among children.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should cease using the bookcase if it is not anchored to the wall and store it out of children's reach. Dania Furniture will provide a free in-home installation of a tip-over restraint kit. If installation is not feasible or a refund is preferred, Dania Furniture will issue a full refund upon return of the bookcase and arrange for its pickup or disposal. The company will also reach out to all purchasers directly.

recalls.metaDescription: Dania Furniture recalls Hayden Bookcase due to tip-over hazards, linked to a child's death. Immediate action recommended for consumers.

recalls.Description: Cinmar is recalling all Frontgate Resort Collection Newport Aluminum Chaises (SKU 67333) and Newport Teak Chaises (SKU 166218) due to safety concerns. These reclining outdoor lounge chairs, available in various colors and finishes, measure approximately 51 inches in length and 31 inches in width. They feature a powder-coated aluminum or teak frame, a chaise-style seat, and a mesh backrest with an adjustable feature that allows for four reclining positions.
recalls.Title: Cinmar Recalls Frontgate Chaise Lounge Chairs Due to Finger Injury Risks
recalls.ConsumerContact: Consumers should immediately stop using the recalled chaise lounge chairs and reach out to Cinmar at 888-298-4651, available Monday through Friday from 8 a.m. to 8 p.m. ET and Saturday from 9:30 a.m. to 6 p.m. For more information, visit www.frontgate.com/newportchaiserecall or go to www.frontgate.com and select the 'Product Recalls' link.

Products.Name: Frontgate Resort Collection Newport Aluminum Chaises

Images.Caption: ecalled Cinmar Frontgate Resort Collection Newport Aluminum Chaise
Recalled Cinmar Frontgate Resort Collection Newport Teak Chaise

Injuries.Name: Cinmar has received three reports of incidents involving consumers' fingers becoming entrapped between the adjustable backrest and the chair frame, resulting in two partial finger amputation injuries, and one finger-crushing injury requiring surgery.

Retailers.Name: Available at Frontgate stores in Georgia, Kentucky, North Carolina, Ohio, and Texas, as well as online at Frontgate.com and in Frontgate catalogs from February 2014 through December 2023, priced between $600 and $1,400.

Importers.Name: Cinmar LLC, of West Chester, Ohio

Hazards.Name: Risk of finger crushing and amputation due to unexpected descent of the backrest.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should stop using the chairs and contact Cinmar for a free repair kit, which includes a new backrest support bar and assembly instructions.

recalls.metaDescription: Cinmar recalls Frontgate chaise lounge chairs due to finger injury risks. Stop using and contact for a free repair kit.

recalls.Description: Global Home USA is recalling its Cedona Natural View six-drawer dressers, model 33117082, due to serious safety concerns. These dressers, measuring approximately 68 inches in length, 18 inches in width, and 36 inches in height, weigh around 238 pounds. A white label on the back displays the model number in black ink. If not securely anchored to the wall, these dressers can pose significant tip-over and entrapment hazards, particularly for children, and do not meet the STURDY Act's safety standards.
recalls.Title: Global Home USA's Cedona Natural View Six-Drawer Dressers Recalled for Tip-Over and Entrapment Risks
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact Rooms To Go at 855-688-0919, available Monday through Friday from 9 a.m. to 4 p.m. ET, or email productcare@roomstogo.com. More information is available at www.cedonadresserrecall.com.

Images.Caption: Recalled Cedona Natural View six-drawer dresser
White label on the back of the recalled dresser

Retailers.Name: Available at Rooms To Go stores nationwide and online at www.roomstogo.com from November 2023 through February 2024 for approximately $1,000

Importers.Name: Global Homes USA, of Charlotte, North Carolina

Hazards.Name: Tip-over and entrapment hazards due to instability if not anchored to the wall

Remedies.Name: Free replacement with delivery and removal of the recalled dresser

recalls.metaDescription: Cedona Natural View six-drawer dressers recalled by Global Home USA due to tip-over risks. Contact Rooms To Go for a free replacement.

recalls.Description: Article has announced a recall of their Spin Swivel Chairs due to a potential fall hazard. These upholstered chairs feature gold-colored stainless steel swivel legs and are available in ivory, gray, orange, and blue. Each chair stands approximately 32.5 inches tall, 32 inches deep, and 30.5 inches wide, making them a stylish addition to any space.
recalls.Title: Recall of Spin Swivel Chairs Due to Fall Risk by Article
recalls.ConsumerContact: Consumers should cease use of the recalled chairs immediately and reach out to Article for a complimentary replacement base along with installation instructions. Contact Article at 888-746-3455 from 6 a.m. to 5 p.m. PT on weekdays, and from 7 a.m. to 5 p.m. PT on weekends. Additional information can be found at service@article.com or by visiting https://www.article.com/c/spin-recall.

Products.Name: Spin Swivel Chairs
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 4,500 (In addition, about 140 were sold in Canada)

Retailers.Name: Available exclusively online at article.com from July 2021 to June 2023 for approximately $700.

Importers.Name: Trademango Solutions Inc., dba Article, of Canada

Hazards.Name: Fall hazard due to potential base breakage

Remedies.Name: Free replacement base and installation instructions

recalls.metaDescription: Article recalls Spin Swivel Chairs due to fall risk. Stop using immediately and contact Article for a free replacement base.

recalls.Description: LFN Limited has recalled the Mill Valley Jr. six-drawer youth dressers due to significant safety concerns. These dressers, available in white or cherry finishes, have model numbers C2192Y-N04 and C2193Y-N04. Measuring approximately 57 ½ inches in length, 18 inches in width, and 35 inches in height, they weigh around 132 pounds. A white label on the back identifies the model numbers. The dressers pose serious tip-over and entrapment hazards if not anchored to a wall, violating federal safety standards under the STURDY Act.
recalls.Title: Recall of Mill Valley Jr. Six-Drawer Youth Dressers Due to Tip-Over Risks
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach Rooms To Go toll-free at 855-688-0919, Monday through Friday from 9 a.m. to 4 p.m. ET, email at productcare@roomstogo.com, or visit www.MillValleyJuniorRecall.com.

Products.Name: Mill Valley Jr. Six-Drawer Youth Dressers

Importers.Name: LFN Limited, of China

Hazards.Name: Tip-over and entrapment hazards due to instability if not anchored to the wall.

Remedies.Name: Stop using the dressers, keep children away, and contact Rooms To Go for a free replacement.

recalls.Description: SMART Technologies is recalling its Electric Height-Adjustable Mobile Stands designed for large interactive flat panel displays due to safety concerns. The affected models include FSE-400 (available worldwide) and FSE-410 (specific to the US and Canada). These stands are characterized by their white color and a four-wheel base. Consumers can find the model number, serial number, and SMART logo on a label located at the back of the stand. It is crucial for users to check if their serial number is part of this recall by visiting the provided online link.
recalls.Title: Recall of SMART Technologies Electric Height-Adjustable Mobile Stands Due to Tip-Over and Entrapment Risks
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers should reach out to SMART Technologies ULC at 888-427-6278 from 6 a.m. to 6 p.m. MT, Monday to Friday. Additional information can be found online at https://support.smarttech.com/docs/general-support-info/en/recalls or by visiting https://www.smarttech.com and clicking on 'RECALLS' under 'Support and training'.

Images.Caption: Recalled Electric Height-Adjustable Mobile Stand for Large Interactive Flat Panel Displays
Images.SourceImageURL: https://www.cpsc.gov/s3fs-public/Recalled Electric Height-Adjustable Mobile Stand for Large Interactive Flat Panel Displays.png

Images.Caption: Location of serial number, model number and SMART logo on the back of the recalled mobile stand
Images.SourceImageURL: https://www.cpsc.gov/s3fs-public/Location of serial number, model number and SMART logo on the back of the recalled mobile stand.png

Injuries.Name: The firm has received one report of the wheelbase welds breaking while the stand was being moved. No injuries have been reported.

Retailers.Name: SMART authorized distributors and resellers that sold directly to businesses across the nation from May 2023 to December 2023, priced at approximately $1,735.

Importers.Name: SMART Technologies ULC, of Canada

Hazards.Name: Tip-over and entrapment hazards due to potential bending or breaking of wheelbase welds

Remedies.Name: Free on-site replacement of the wheelbase for affected consumers

recalls.metaDescription: SMART Technologies recalls Electric Height-Adjustable Mobile Stands due to safety hazards. Check your model for potential risks.

recalls.Description: Clark Associates is recalling Lancaster Table & Seating brand Allegro plastic side chairs due to a potential fall hazard. These chairs, designed for outdoor use, were available in 14 vibrant colors and are marked with the brand name on the underside. Each chair measures approximately 17 inches in width and 33 inches in height, constructed from glass-reinforced polypropylene.
recalls.Title: Clark Associates Issues Recall for Allegro Plastic Side Chairs Due to Fall Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach Clark Associates at 844-895-5694 from 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday, or email allegro@lancastertableandseating.com. More information is available online at lancastertableandseating.com/recalls/allegro-side-chair-recall/ or by visiting www.therestaurantstore.com and www.webstaurantstore.com and clicking on 'Safety Recall' at the bottom of the page.

Images.Caption: Recalled Lancaster Table & Seating Allegro plastic side chair
Recalled Lancaster Table & Seating Allegro plastic side chair stamp on underside of plastic side chair

Injuries.Name: The firm has received 16 reports of chairs breaking apart while in use, resulting in two injuries.

Retailers.Name: Available through Clark Pro and Clark National Accounts across the country, as well as online at www.webstaurantstore.com and www.therestaurantstore.com from November 2020 to August 2023, priced between $45 and $66.

Importers.Name: Clark Core Services LLC, d/b/a Clark Associates, of Lancaster, Pennsylvania

Hazards.Name: Risk of fall due to potential breakage

Remedies.Name: Stop using the chairs immediately and contact Clark Associates for a full refund or store credit. Consumers must submit a photo showing 'DO NOT USE' clearly marked on the chairs and dispose of them. Clark Associates will reach out to all known purchasers.

recalls.metaDescription: Clark Associates recalls Allegro plastic side chairs due to fall hazards. Contact for refunds and safety details.

recalls.Description: TJX has issued a recall for foldable bistro set chairs that were sold as part of three- or five-piece sets. These chairs, available under the Nautica and House & Garden brands, feature wooden frames and rope seating in various colors including gray, turquoise, and pink. The sets include a small wooden table and two to four chairs, with some chairs sold individually. The affected products were also marketed online as handcrafted in Vietnam or as part of an indoor-outdoor furniture set.
recalls.Title: TJX Recalls Foldable Bistro Set Chairs Due to Fall Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For more details, consumers can reach TJX toll-free at 888-520-0157 from 7 a.m. to 6 p.m. CT, Monday through Friday, or visit https://www.recallrtr.com/woodandropechair. Additionally, recalls can be found on the websites of HomeGoods, Marshalls, T.J. Maxx, Homesense, and Sierra.

Images.Caption: Recalled TJX Foldable wood and rope bistro set chairs
Recalled TJX Foldable wood and rope bistro set chairs (blue)
Recalled TJX Foldable wood and rope bistro set chairs (gray)
Recalled TJX Foldable wood and rope bistro set chairs (sand)

Injuries.Name: The firm has received eight reports of the wooden frame of the chair breaking or collapsing when a consumer was seated in the chair, resulting in six reports of minor injuries, including bruising and an ankle strain.

Retailers.Name: Available at Marshalls, T.J. Maxx, HomeGoods, Homesense, and Sierra stores across the country, as well as online at Homegoods.com, TJmaxx.tjx.com, Marshalls.com, and Sierra.com from March 2020 to September 2023, priced between $130 and $350 for sets and $40 to $80 for individual chairs.

Importers.Name: The TJX Companies Inc., of Framingham, Massachusetts

Hazards.Name: Risk of fall due to potential breakage or collapse of the wooden chair frame.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should cease use of the recalled chairs and contact TJX for a full refund and disposal instructions. Alternatively, chairs can be returned to any HomeGoods, Marshalls, T.J. Maxx, Homesense, or Sierra store for a complete refund.

recalls.metaDescription: TJX recalls foldable bistro set chairs due to a fall hazard. Stop using and contact for refunds or return to stores.
